jobs.label: fix
jobs.prompt:
Improve the guardrail bounce error messages in the dispatch pipeline so they tell the caller exactly what to fix.

Current behavior: Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow_pipeline.py line ~179 appends `governance:{gov_result.blocked_reason}` to a blocked list. The model or operator sees a terse string like 'governance:Secrets detected in prompt' with no guidance.

Fix: when a governance bounce occurs, return a structured message that includes:
1. What was blocked and why (already present in blocked_reason)
2. Which job label triggered it
3. A concrete remediation hint (e.g. 'Remove or redact the secret from job \'build_step\' prompt before resubmitting')

The governance block result comes from Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/governance.py lines ~146-156. Look at what fields are available on gov_result and surface them clearly. The output of pre_dispatch (the PipelineGate) should carry enough context that the caller can fix the issue without guessing.
